title.value: A survey on automatic generation of medical imaging reports based on deep learning
title.evidence: A survey on automatic generation of medical imaging reports based on deep learning

authors.evidence: Ting Pang1*, Peigao Li1 and Lijie Zhao1

year.evidence: Received: 19 February 2023 Accepted: 9 May 2023 Published online: 18 May 2023

abstract.value: Recent advances in deep learning have shown great potential for the automatic generation of medical imaging reports. Deep learning techniques, inspired by image captioning, have made significant progress in the field of diagnostic report generation. This paper provides a comprehensive overview of recent research efforts in deep learning-based medical imaging report generation and proposes future directions in this field. First, we summarize and analyze the dataset, architecture, application, and evaluation of deep learning-based medical imaging report generation. Specially, we survey the deep learning architectures used in diagnostic report generation, including hierarchical RNN-based frameworks, attention-based frameworks, and reinforcement learning-based frameworks. In addition, we identify potential challenges and suggest future research directions to support clinical applications and decision-making using medical imaging report generation systems.
abstract.evidence: Recent advances in deep learning have shown great potential for the automatic generation of medical imaging reports...

doi.notes: No DOI provided in the paper.

problem_statement.value: The task of writing diagnostic reports for medical images is time-consuming and requires skilled professionals, creating a need for automatic report generation to ease the workload of radiologists.
problem_statement.evidence: The automatic generation of diagnostic reports is inspired by image captioning... However, generating diagnostic reports is a challenging task due to the complexity and diversity of objects in medical images.

value.name: IU X-Ray
value.description: Chest X-ray images paired with their corresponding diagnostic reports.

value.name: ChestX-ray14
value.description: Contains 112,120 frontal-view X-ray images of 30,805 unique patients with 14 kinds of lung diseases.

value.name: CheXpert
value.description: Chest radiographs of 65,240 patients with both frontal and lateral views available.

value.name: MIMIC-CXR
value.description: A large publicly available dataset of chest radiographs in DICOM format with free-text radiology reports.

value.name: PadChest
value.description: Chest X-ray dataset obtained from 67,000 patients with 160,000 images.

data_sets.evidence: The current public datasets have greatly contributed to the development of deep learning for medical imaging report generation...
data_sets.notes: Includes both public and private datasets.

value.name: Hierarchical RNN-based framework
value.description: Utilizes a hierarchical LSTM consisting of a sentence LSTM and a word LSTM for generating long reports.

value.name: Attention-based framework
value.description: Implements attention mechanisms to enhance image alignment and generate sharper joint image/report attention maps.

value.name: Reinforcement Learning-based framework
value.description: Employs a multi-agent system to optimize report generation through rewards based on normality and abnormality.

architectures.evidence: This paper presents a survey of recent studies on deep learning-based medical imaging report generation, organized into four sections...
architectures.notes: Highlights various frameworks for report generation.

value.name: BLEU
value.description: Measures the similarity between the generated report and the ground truth report.

value.name: ROUGE
value.description: Measures the longest common subsequence between generated and reference descriptions.

value.name: METEOR
value.description: Extends BLEU by adopting F-score of precision and recall.

value.name: CIDER
value.description: Measures cosine similarity between n-gram TF-IDF representations of generated and reference reports.

evaluation_metrics.evidence: BLEU, ROUGE, METEOR and CIDER are commonly used evaluation metrics for medical image report generation...
evaluation_metrics.notes: These metrics are adapted from machine translation and text summarization.

value: Deep learning-based automatic report generation can significantly reduce the workload of radiologists.
The use of semantic information improves the interpretability of deep learning models in medical imaging.
Attention mechanisms enhance the quality of generated reports by focusing on salient features in images.
key_findings.evidence: This paper presents a survey of recent studies on deep learning-based medical imaging report generation...

value: Imbalanced datasets leading to biased report generation.
Lack of standardization in evaluation metrics.
Need for interdisciplinary collaboration between medical professionals and AI experts.
challenges_identified.evidence: Despite the significant progress made in medical imaging report generation based on deep learning, this section aims to highlight the unresolved issues...

value: Establish public databases with diverse imaging modalities.
Develop private datasets to enhance clinical practice.
Create specific evaluation metrics tailored for the medical domain.
future_directions.evidence: To improve the evaluation process, it is necessary to design more specific metrics in the medical domain...

conclusion.value: Automatic generation of diagnostic reports from medical images can significantly reduce the workload of report writing and improve the interpretability of deep learning models.
conclusion.evidence: Automatic generation of diagnostic reports from medical images can significantly reduce the workload of report writing...

main_manuscript.evidence: TP and PL wrote the main manuscript text...
